checks.a_analysis: The output contains sections and paragraphs that directly correspond to the source PDF's title page, table of contents, purpose, scope, history, and glossary entries. No fabricated text or sections were identified. The metadata title and image alt text are expected AI-generated additions.

There are no phone numbers or email addresses in the provided output snippet. The source PDF contains several URLs (e.g., https://cve.mitre.org, https://csrc.nist.gov, https://nvd.nist.gov, https://it.nc.gov/...) which are correctly omitted from the snippet due to the token budget truncation (indicated by '73 sections omitted'). No fabricated URLs or contact details were found in the visible sections.

The output indicates a page count of 78, matching the source. The provided sections cover the beginning (pages 1-3) and end (pages 77-78) of the document. The structure of the glossary entries matches the source PDF's content at both ends of the glossary (starting with '3-Way Handshake' and 'A' terms, ending with 'W' through 'Z' terms).
The document does not contain traditional data tables. The Table of Contents is converted to a list format, which faithfully represents all entries and page numbers from the source.

checks.a_analysis: A thorough review of the glossary entries in the output (e.g., 'Zero Trust (ZT)', 'Worm', 'Abstraction') against the source OCR confirms that the definitions are captured completely. No paragraphs or significant sentences within the included sections were omitted.

checks.a_analysis: The output preserves the wording and meaning of the source text. Minor OCR artifacts from the source, such as 'Actual Cost of Work Perform' (which appears on page 3 of the original PDF despite being corrected to 'Performed' on page 4), are faithfully reproduced without unauthorized alterations.

checks.a_analysis: An examination of the output document confirms that no text blocks are repeated. The content flows logically from the cover page through the table of contents and into the glossary definitions, matching the progression of the source PDF without redundant sections or paragraphs.

The heading hierarchy is preserved and logically structured. The document uses H1 for major titles (e.g., 'State of North Carolina', 'Table of Contents'), H2 for primary document sections (e.g., 'Purpose', 'Scope', 'Glossary of Terms'), and H3 for glossary letter markers and subsections (e.g., 'General', 'A', 'X', 'Y', 'Z'). This provides a consistent and accessible navigation structure for screen readers without any skipped levels.

The source document primarily consists of a table of contents and a glossary of terms. No traditional grid-based data tables are present in the provided source material. The Table of Contents is appropriately converted into a semantic list, which is an acceptable structural transformation for accessibility.

The output maintains a logical reading order that follows the visual and conceptual flow of the original document. Content blocks like headings, paragraphs, and images on the title page are ordered sensibly, and the sequence of glossary terms corresponds exactly to the alphabetical order in the source.

The Table of Contents is correctly converted from a visual list with leader lines into a structured unordered list in the JSON output. This preserves the relationships between the topics and their respective page numbers while improving document semantics.

Based on the provided output sections (1, 2, 3, 77, 78), there are no URLs present that required link conversion. Glossary entries in these sections do not contain external links. Therefore, no link fabrication or missing link defects are observed in the audited portions.
